items.name: Easyduino
items.source_story_title: Easyduino: Open Source PCB Devboards for KiCad
items.summary: Open-source KiCad devboard templates for remixing existing PCB designs.
items.why_included: Commenters praised it as a reusable PCB design system for fast board tweaks.
items.evidence: The thread said it was the kind of PCB design base people had wanted for years and noted how agonizing Eagle-to-KiCad import can be.

items.name: Super ZSNES
items.source_story_title: Super ZSNES – GPU Powered SNES Emulator
items.summary: GPU-powered SNES emulator focused on visual upgrades and modern rendering.
items.why_included: HN commenters dug into the emulator’s rendering tricks, accuracy tradeoffs, and Unity implementation.
items.evidence: The discussion highlighted high-res Mode 7, texture replacements, shaders, and the accuracy bar set by CPU emulators like higan.

items.name: Utilyze
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Utilyze – an open source GPU monitoring tool more accurate than nvtop
items.summary: Open-source GPU monitor that measures real throughput instead of raw utilization.
items.why_included: HN discussion liked the clearer compute-versus-memory view and the planned process and power stats.
items.evidence: The thread said nvidia-smi and nvtop-style utilization can show 100% while actual compute is far lower.

items.name: Quarkdown
items.summary: Markdown-based document system pushing text docs toward typesetting.
items.why_included: Commenters compared it favorably with Typst, Quarto, Pandoc, and LaTeX workflows.
items.evidence: HN said Quarkdown is a strict upgrade over typing LaTeX directly and placed it alongside Typst, Quarto, MyST, and Pandoc.

items.name: Flipdiscs
items.summary: Flip-disc display project that turns real-time data into kinetic visuals.
items.why_included: HN comments praised the sound, the art installations, and the live tide-driven animation.
items.evidence: Commenters highlighted BREAKFAST installations and the real-time API data driving the wave-like motion.

items.name: Dirac
items.source_story_title: Show HN: OSS Agent I built topped the TerminalBench on Gemini-3-flash-preview
items.summary: Open-source agent CLI harness that scored well on TerminalBench.
items.why_included: Commenters were impressed by the benchmark result and the no-cheating setup.
items.evidence: The thread said Dirac scored 65.2% on TerminalBench 2.0, beating Google’s official result and Junie CLI.

items.name: Barman
items.source_story_title: Pgbackrest is no longer being maintained
items.summary: PostgreSQL backup manager commenters praised as a mature disaster-recovery tool.
items.why_included: The pgBackRest thread repeatedly pointed people toward Barman as a dependable alternative.
items.evidence: Commenters said Barman had worked successfully at scale and handled long-term restore testing well.

items.name: WAL-G
items.source_story_title: Pgbackrest is no longer being maintained
items.summary: PostgreSQL backup and restore tool discussed as a common pgBackRest alternative.
items.why_included: HN commenters compared it directly with pgBackRest for PITR and compressed backups.
items.evidence: The thread described WAL-G as a streaming backup option with praise for portability and non-exclusive base backups.

items.name: Carbon Copy Cloner
items.summary: macOS backup app commenters use for Time Machine replacements.
items.why_included: The macOS 27 thread cited it as a practical backup path when AFP and Time Machine setups break.
items.evidence: Users said they had success with Synology NAS plus Carbon Copy Cloner and other modern backup workflows.

items.name: restic
items.summary: Cross-platform backup program suggested as a modern Time Machine alternative.
items.why_included: HN commenters named restic-based backups as a working replacement for legacy AFP setups.
items.evidence: The macOS thread grouped restic with SMB/ext4 and other successful backup workflows.

items.name: Netatalk
items.summary: Open-source AFP file server used for legacy Mac backup compatibility.
items.why_included: Commenters brought it up as the legacy bridge for older Time Machine and AFP environments.
items.evidence: The macOS discussion explicitly mentioned Netatalk/AFP-based backups as the old setup people may need to migrate away from.

items.name: OpenRouter
items.source_story_title: GitHub Copilot is moving to usage-based billing
items.summary: Unified model gateway cited as a cheaper Copilot alternative.
items.why_included: The Copilot billing thread repeatedly compared GitHub Copilot against OpenRouter and direct model APIs.
items.evidence: Commenters said the new pricing makes Copilot much less attractive than OpenRouter, direct APIs, and other alternatives.
